assessment.title: JoraIQ Company-Based Assessment
assessment.description: Placement-readiness assessment covering hard DSA, 30-minute SQL, OOPs design and medium-to-hard Core CS MCQs.

questions.title: Incident SLA Scheduling with Dependencies
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
An enterprise support team handles incidents where some incidents can start only after prerequisite incidents are completed. Each incident has a resolution time and an SLA deadline. One engineer works on one incident at a time. The team wants to maximize how many incidents are completed within SLA while respecting dependencies.

Problem Statement
You are given n incidents numbered from 0 to n-1. dependencies[i] = [a, b] means incident a depends on incident b, so b must be completed before a. durations[i] is the time needed to resolve incident i. deadlines[i] is the latest completion time for incident i to be counted as on-time. You may resolve incidents in any valid order. Return the maximum number of incidents completed on or before their deadlines. If the dependency graph has a cycle, return -1.

open_test_cases.purpose: First incident misses SLA but chain can continue

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Dependent incident misses by one unit
Optimal order is not input order
Large dependent job may be skipped for SLA score
At least one SLA must be missed
Valid topo order with all on-time
Prerequisite can miss SLA but unlocks others
Small jobs before long job can still be optimal

test_cases.purpose: First incident misses SLA but chain can continue
Dependent incident misses by one unit
Optimal order is not input order
Large dependent job may be skipped for SLA score
At least one SLA must be missed
Valid topo order with all on-time
Prerequisite can miss SLA but unlocks others
Small jobs before long job can still be optimal

questions.title: Same-Day Delivery Route Optimization
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
A logistics company runs a same-day delivery system in a large city.

There are multiple delivery hubs and thousands of customer locations. Roads between locations have travel times. However, some roads are affected by traffic and can only be used after a certain time.

Each package has a pickup hub, a customer delivery location, and a latest delivery deadline.

A delivery partner starts from location 0 at time 0. The partner can carry only one package at a time.

For every package, the partner must travel from the current location to the package pickup hub, pick up the package instantly, travel from pickup hub to customer location, and deliver before or exactly at the deadline.

The partner may choose the order of packages. You need to find the maximum number of packages that can be delivered on time.

Problem Statement
You are given n locations numbered from 0 to n-1, m directed roads, and k packages.

Each road is [u, v, travelTime, openTime]. This means there is a directed road from u to v. The road can only be used at or after openTime. If the delivery partner reaches location u before openTime, they must wait until openTime. Arrival time at v is max(currentTime, openTime) + travelTime.

Each package is [pickupLocation, deliveryLocation, deadline].

The delivery partner starts at location 0 at time 0. Return the maximum number of packages that can be delivered on time.

Input Example
n = 6
roads = [[0,1,4,0],[1,2,3,0],[2,3,5,10],[0,4,2,0],[4,3,10,0],[3,5,2,0],[1,5,20,0],[4,2,6,5],[3,2,1,0]]
packages = [[1,3,18],[4,5,20],[2,5,25],[1,5,30]]

Expected Output
2

Note: roads are directed. The example includes road [3,2,1,0] so a route can continue from the first delivery area to another pickup hub. Without that directed connector, only one package is deliverable.

open_test_cases.purpose: Sample with time-dependent roads and two deliverable packages
Must wait for a road to open

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Late-opening fast edge loses to slower usable route
Cycle route can complete all packages
Delivery exactly at deadline is valid
Time-dependent shortest paths change with current time

test_cases.purpose: Sample with time-dependent roads and two deliverable packages
Must wait for a road to open
Late-opening fast edge loses to slower usable route
Cycle route can complete all packages
Delivery exactly at deadline is valid
Time-dependent shortest paths change with current time

questions.title: Global Backup Deduplication Range Analytics
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
A backup platform receives files from many machines. Each file is represented by chunk hashes. Storage leaders want to answer audit questions: within a selected range of files, how many distinct chunks would be stored if deduplication were applied only to that range?

Problem Statement
You are given files, where files[i] is the list of chunk hashes present in file i. A chunk repeated inside the same file should be counted once for that file. You are also given queries, where each query [left, right] asks for the number of unique chunk hashes appearing in files left through right inclusive. Return one answer per query in the same order.
constraints: Total number of chunks across all files <= 200000

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Repeated chunk and empty range end

test_cases.purpose: Repeated chunk and empty range end

questions.title: Branching Versioned Key-Value Store
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
A design platform allows users to create alternative versions of the same project. A new version can be created from any previous version. Each new version changes exactly one design parameter while inheriting all other parameters from its base version.

Problem Statement
Design a branching versioned key-value store. Version 0 is empty. set(baseVersion, key, value) creates a new version from baseVersion, updates key to value in that new version, and returns the new version number. get(version, key) returns the value of key visible in that version. If the key does not exist in that version, return "NULL".
constraints: Version numbers are created sequentially starting from 1
A parent-chain lookup solution is acceptable for this version
questions.expected_time_complexity: O(depth) per get, O(1) per set

open_test_cases.purpose: Sibling branches do not share later updates

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Branch isolation with inherited root value
Two branches overwrite same key differently
Child update not visible in parent

test_cases.purpose: Sibling branches do not share later updates
Branch isolation with inherited root value
Two branches overwrite same key differently
Child update not visible in parent

questions.title: Blinkit Dark Store Replenishment Risk
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Blinkit operates dark stores that hold fast-moving grocery SKUs. Store managers need to identify active products that may go out of stock soon based on current inventory, reorder rules, and recent demand.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to return active store-SKU combinations that need replenishment. Assume the current date is 2026-06-15. Use the latest inventory snapshot for each store and SKU. Calculate average daily units sold from completed sales in the last 7 days. A SKU should be flagged if current on-hand units are below the SKU reorder point or if days of cover is less than 3. Exclude inactive stores and discontinued SKUs. Treat SKUs with no recent sales as 0 average daily sales and do not flag them only because days of cover is null.

Required Output
Return store_id, store_name, sku_id, sku_name, on_hand_units, avg_daily_units_sold, days_of_cover, and reorder_point.
expected_approach: Find the latest inventory snapshot per store and SKU
Filter active stores and non-discontinued SKUs
Aggregate completed sales from the last 7 days
Calculate average daily units sold and days of cover
Flag rows below reorder point or below 3 days of cover

questions.title: PayU Settlement Reconciliation Mismatch
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
PayU settles captured payments to merchants after deducting gateway fees and tax. Finance needs to identify successful transactions whose settlement amount does not reconcile with what should have been paid to the merchant.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to return captured transactions whose settlement due date has passed as of 2026-06-15 and whose settlement is missing or mismatched. Expected settlement amount is transaction amount minus gateway fee minus tax. Sum all settlement payouts made for the transaction. Flag a transaction if no settlement exists or if the absolute difference between expected and settled amount is greater than 1. Exclude inactive merchants and non-captured transactions.

Required Output
Return merchant_id, merchant_name, txn_id, txn_amount, expected_settlement_amount, settled_amount, settlement_gap, and settlement_status.
expected_approach: Filter active merchants and captured transactions past settlement due date
Calculate expected settlement as amount minus fee minus tax
LEFT JOIN and aggregate settlement payouts by transaction
Calculate settlement gap between expected and actual settled amount
Flag missing settlements or mismatches greater than 1

questions.title: Salesforce Renewal Expansion Opportunity
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Salesforce customer success wants to find accounts that are good renewal expansion candidates. These accounts have contracts ending soon, strong product adoption, and no unresolved critical support issues.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to return active accounts with an active non-trial contract ending in the next 60 days from 2026-06-15. Count distinct non-internal licensed users who used the product in the last 30 days from 2026-06-15. A customer is an expansion candidate if utilization is at least 80% of purchased seats and the account has no open critical support tickets. Exclude internal users from usage counts.

Required Output
Return account_id, account_name, plan_type, seats_purchased, active_users_30d, utilization_percentage, and contract_end_date.
expected_approach: Filter active non-trial contracts ending in the next 60 days
Exclude internal users from usage counts
Count distinct licensed users active in the last 30 days
Exclude accounts with open critical support tickets
Flag accounts with utilization at least 80% of purchased seats

questions.title: Atlassian Jira Issue Workflow Engine
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Atlassian Jira is used by software teams to track work items such as Bugs, Tasks and Feature Requests. Each issue type can follow a different workflow. For example, a Bug may go from Open to In Progress to Fixed to Verified to Closed, while a Task may simply go from To Do to In Progress to Done. The platform needs a flexible workflow engine where new issue types can be added later without changing the core engine.

Problem Statement
Design the core classes/interfaces for creating issues, moving an issue to its next valid state, rejecting invalid transitions, and returning the current state. Provide concise pseudocode or class code for at least two issue workflows. Do not include database, UI, networking, or authentication code.

domain_rules: Bug issues follow Open -> In Progress -> Fixed -> Verified -> Closed.
Tasks follow To Do -> In Progress -> Done.
Feature Requests follow Draft -> Review -> Approved -> In Development -> Released.
Invalid transitions must be rejected with a clear error or failed result.
required_components: A common Issue abstraction or interface
Separate workflow behavior for at least two issue types
A WorkflowEngine or service that delegates transition rules
A way to query current issue state
design_constraints: Adding a new issue type should not require editing existing engine transition code.
Avoid large if/else or switch blocks on issue type inside the core engine.
Keep issue data encapsulated; external code should not mutate state directly.
acceptance_signals: Uses State, Strategy, or equivalent polymorphic design.
Demonstrates at least two issue workflows with different transitions.
Shows how a new workflow can be registered or introduced with minimal change.
red_flags: Single procedural function with hard-coded issue type checks
Public mutable state with no transition validation
Design cannot add a new workflow without modifying existing workflow engine logic

starter_code.python: # Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.javascript: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.java: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.cpp: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.

questions.title: Razorpay Multi-Mode Payment Processing System
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Razorpay allows merchants to accept payments through different modes such as Card, UPI and Wallet. Each payment mode has its own validation and processing rules. For example, Card payments need card detail validation, UPI payments need UPI ID validation, and Wallet payments need sufficient balance. The checkout flow should remain simple and should not change whenever a new payment mode is added.

Problem Statement
Design the core classes/interfaces for validating a payment, processing it, returning success/failure, and adding new modes without modifying checkout logic. Provide concise pseudocode or class code for at least three payment modes. Do not include external gateways, persistence, UI, or authentication code.

domain_rules: Card payments require card detail validation before processing.
UPI payments require VPA validation before processing.
Wallet payments require sufficient balance before debit.
required_components: A PaymentMethod interface or abstract base
Concrete payment implementations for at least three modes
CheckoutService depending on the abstraction rather than concrete classes
Validation and processing methods with success/failure result
design_constraints: Adding a new payment mode should not require modifying CheckoutService.
Do not place all payment rules inside one if/else chain.
Payment-specific validation should live with the payment strategy.
Checkout flow should orchestrate payment without knowing internal mode rules.
acceptance_signals: Demonstrates polymorphic process or pay method.
Returns consistent payment result objects or status values.
Handles validation failure separately from payment processing failure.
red_flags: CheckoutService switches on payment type for every rule
New payment mode requires editing existing checkout logic
Validation and processing responsibilities are mixed across unrelated classes

starter_code.python: # Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.javascript: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.java: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.cpp: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.

questions.title: Canva Design Export System
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Canva allows users to create designs such as posters, presentations, documents and social media creatives. After creating a design, users can export it in different formats such as PDF, PNG and 3D preview. Each format has different export rules. For example, PDF export may need document metadata, PNG export may need image resolution, and 3D preview export may need model-specific settings. The core design file should not contain all export logic because new formats may be added in the future.

Problem Statement
Design the core classes/interfaces for exporting a design file into different formats, returning success/failure, handling unsupported formats, and adding new exporters without changing the design file class. Provide concise pseudocode or class code for at least three exporters. Do not include file storage, rendering engines, UI, or authentication code.

domain_rules: PDF export should use document metadata.
PNG export should use raster resolution settings.
3D preview export should use model-specific or mesh-specific settings.
Unsupported formats must return a controlled failure instead of crashing.
required_components: A DesignFile domain object that does not implement every format directly
An Exporter interface or abstract base
Concrete exporters for at least three formats
An ExportService, registry, or factory for selecting exporters
A standard export result with status, format, and message or output path
design_constraints: Adding a new format should require adding a new exporter, not modifying DesignFile.
Avoid format-specific if/else chains in the DesignFile class.
Keep export settings explicit rather than hidden global state.
Separate validation of unsupported formats from export execution.
acceptance_signals: Uses composition so DesignFile delegates export behavior.
Shows concrete exporter classes with different behavior.
Provides a clear extension point for future formats.
Handles success and unsupported-format failure consistently.
red_flags: DesignFile has one method with switch/if branches for all formats
Adding a new format requires changing existing DesignFile logic

questions.title: HTML
questions.prompt: A team is building a checkout form. Screen-reader users must understand each input, validation messages should be associated with the right field, and the browser should provide useful mobile keyboards. Which implementation is the best baseline?

options.label: A
options.text: Use semantic form controls with label elements, suitable input types, required attributes, and aria-describedby for help/error text

options.label: B
options.text: Use div elements for all fields and rely on placeholder text as the only field description

options.label: C
options.text: Use contenteditable spans for every input so the layout is easier to style

options.label: D
options.text: Put all labels in a separate paragraph above the form without connecting them to inputs

questions.explanation: Semantic controls, explicit labels, input types and described-by relationships improve accessibility, validation and mobile input behavior.

questions.title: HTML
questions.prompt: A product listing page renders many below-the-fold product images. The first viewport should load quickly, but image dimensions must still be reserved to avoid layout shift. Which HTML approach is most appropriate?

options.label: A
options.text: Remove all alt attributes so the browser downloads less text

options.label: B
options.text: Add width and height attributes and use loading="lazy" for below-the-fold images

options.label: C
options.text: Set every image as a CSS background even when the image is meaningful content

options.label: D
options.text: Use only high-resolution images and let the browser guess their rendered size

questions.explanation: Explicit dimensions reduce layout shift, and lazy loading defers non-critical image downloads.

questions.title: HTML
questions.prompt: A documentation site has a header, primary navigation, article content, related links and footer. Keyboard and screen-reader users need to jump between major page regions reliably. Which markup strategy best supports this?

options.label: A
options.text: Use only div elements and style them visually to look like regions

options.label: B
options.text: Put the entire page inside one table because it creates clear rows and columns

options.label: C
options.text: Use multiple h1 elements as the only navigation mechanism

options.label: D
options.text: Use header, nav, main, aside and footer elements for their matching page regions

questions.explanation: HTML landmark elements expose meaningful regions to assistive technologies and improve document structure.

questions.title: HTML
questions.prompt: A landing page includes a non-critical analytics script and an interactive menu script that needs the DOM to be parsed before it runs. The team wants to avoid blocking initial HTML parsing. Which script attribute is safest for the menu script?

options.label: A
options.text: async, because it always preserves execution order and waits for the DOM

options.label: B
options.text: nomodule, because it forces modern browsers to execute the script earlier

options.label: C
options.text: No attribute, because parser-blocking scripts always improve first render

options.label: D
options.text: defer, because it downloads during parsing and executes after the document is parsed in order

questions.explanation: defer avoids parser blocking and runs after parsing, which is appropriate for DOM-dependent scripts that should keep order.

questions.title: HTML
questions.prompt: A modal close control is implemented as a clickable icon. It must be keyboard accessible, announce its purpose, and work consistently with form behavior. Which markup is best?

options.label: A
options.text: Use a button element with type="button" and an accessible label such as aria-label="Close dialog"

options.label: B
options.text: Use a span with an onclick handler and no keyboard event handling

options.label: C
options.text: Use an anchor without href because it looks like a clickable element

options.label: D
options.text: Use an img tag and rely on the file name to describe the action

questions.explanation: button provides native keyboard and accessibility behavior; type="button" prevents accidental form submission.

questions.title: CSS
questions.prompt: A card grid should show one column on phones, two on tablets, and as many 280px cards as fit on desktop without hardcoding every breakpoint. Which CSS Grid pattern best fits this requirement?

options.label: A
options.text: float every card left and set a fixed pixel width for the container

options.label: B
options.text: position every card absolutely with left and top values

options.label: C
options.text: grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr))

options.label: D
options.text: Use display: table and manually add empty cells for spacing

questions.explanation: auto-fit with minmax creates a responsive grid that adapts to available space without many explicit breakpoints.

questions.title: CSS
questions.prompt: A component style `.card .title { color: black; }` is unexpectedly overriding a theme utility `.text-primary { color: blue; }` on the same title element. What is the most likely reason?

options.label: A
options.text: CSS ignores class selectors when they are used for color

options.label: B
options.text: The browser applies styles alphabetically by property name

options.label: C
options.text: The title element cannot receive inherited color

options.label: D
options.text: The descendant selector has higher specificity than a single class selector

questions.explanation: `.card .title` has two class selectors, so it has higher specificity than `.text-primary`.

questions.title: CSS
questions.prompt: A dropdown has z-index: 9999 but still appears behind a sibling panel. Its parent has transform applied for animation. What is the most likely cause?

options.label: A
options.text: z-index only works on table elements

options.label: B
options.text: Dropdowns cannot be layered over sibling panels in CSS

options.label: C
options.text: The transformed parent creates a stacking context, limiting how the child stacks against outside elements

options.label: D
options.text: The browser ignores all z-index values above 100

questions.explanation: Properties like transform can create a new stacking context, so the child cannot escape the parent context by using a large z-index.

questions.title: CSS
questions.prompt: A toolbar uses flex-direction: row. The team wants buttons spread horizontally with equal free space between them and all buttons vertically centered. Which properties match the requirement?

options.label: A
options.text: align-content: space-between and justify-items: center

options.label: B
options.text: justify-content: space-between and align-items: center

options.label: C
options.text: text-align: justify and vertical-align: middle

options.label: D
options.text: float: left and line-height: 0

questions.explanation: In a row flex container, justify-content controls the main horizontal axis and align-items controls the cross vertical axis.

questions.title: CSS
questions.prompt: A page loads a custom font late, causing visible text movement and layout shift. Which CSS strategy best reduces the user-visible impact while keeping text readable?

options.label: A
options.text: Use font-display: swap or optional and choose a fallback font with similar metrics

options.label: B
options.text: Hide all text until every font file has downloaded

options.label: C
options.text: Use z-index on the body element to prevent font changes

options.label: D
options.text: Convert all text to images so fonts are never loaded

questions.explanation: font-display and metric-compatible fallbacks reduce invisible text and minimize reflow when the web font arrives.

questions.title: JavaScript
questions.prompt: A click handler starts a long CPU-heavy loop and the page becomes unresponsive until the loop finishes. Network requests are not the bottleneck. What is the best explanation?

options.label: A
options.text: The loop blocks the main thread, preventing rendering and event handling until it completes

options.label: B
options.text: JavaScript always runs each function on a separate CPU thread automatically

options.label: C
options.text: CSS specificity is preventing the click event from bubbling

options.label: D
options.text: HTTP caching is forcing the browser to skip user input

questions.explanation: Long synchronous JavaScript blocks the main thread, so the browser cannot paint or process other events.

questions.title: JavaScript
questions.prompt: A function creates a private counter variable and returns increment and reset functions that keep using that variable after the outer function has returned. What JavaScript concept enables this?

options.label: A
options.text: Hoisting, because all variables are converted into global variables

options.label: B
options.text: Event bubbling, because functions move upward through the DOM

options.label: C
options.text: CSS inheritance, because styles are inherited by child elements

options.label: D
options.text: Closure, because the returned functions retain access to their lexical environment

questions.explanation: A closure lets inner functions remember and access variables from the outer lexical scope after the outer function finishes.

questions.title: JavaScript
questions.prompt: An async function awaits a fetch call. When the server returns HTTP 500, the code still enters the success path because fetch resolved. What should the code do?

options.label: A
options.text: Assume fetch rejects for every HTTP error status and remove all status checks

options.label: B
options.text: Wrap only JSON.parse in try/catch and ignore the HTTP status

options.label: C
options.text: Check response.ok or status after await fetch and throw or handle an application error for non-2xx responses

options.label: D
options.text: Use setTimeout before fetch so all server errors disappear

questions.explanation: fetch rejects mainly for network failures; HTTP error statuses still resolve and must be checked explicitly.

questions.title: JavaScript
questions.prompt: A UI state array is sorted in place using state.items.sort(). A later comparison fails to detect a change correctly because the original array reference was mutated. What is the safer approach?

options.label: A
options.text: Use sort twice so the original reference becomes new

options.label: B
options.text: Convert the array to a string and mutate the string instead

options.label: C
options.text: Create a copy first, such as [...state.items].sort(), and store the new array

options.label: D
options.text: Ignore reference equality because mutation never affects rendering

questions.explanation: Many UI patterns rely on immutable updates and reference changes; copying before sorting avoids mutating existing state.

questions.title: JavaScript
questions.prompt: A search box calls the API on every keypress. Fast typing sends many requests and causes flickering results. The product wants to wait until the user pauses typing before searching. Which technique fits best?

options.label: A
options.text: Debounce the search call so it runs after a short period without new input

options.label: B
options.text: Throttle the call to run exactly once every animation frame forever

options.label: C
options.text: Make the input readonly so no requests are sent

options.label: D
options.text: Send all keypresses and sort responses alphabetically

questions.explanation: Debouncing waits for a pause in input, which is ideal for typeahead/search requests.

questions.title: Cloud Computing
questions.prompt: A web API has low CPU but high p95 latency because requests wait in a queue for database connections. CPU-based autoscaling does not add enough instances. Which scaling signal is more appropriate?

options.label: A
options.text: Average CPU only, because latency should never be used for autoscaling

options.label: B
options.text: The number of files in the application repository

options.label: C
options.text: The amount of unused disk space on developer laptops

options.label: D
options.text: Request queue length, active request concurrency, p95 latency or a custom saturation metric

questions.explanation: When saturation is caused by waiting and queueing, autoscaling should use request and latency signals instead of CPU alone.

questions.title: Cloud Computing
questions.prompt: A production application runs on a single virtual machine in one availability zone. It has nightly backups. What risk remains?

options.label: A
options.text: A VM or zone failure can still cause downtime because backups support recovery, not active high availability

options.label: B
options.text: Backups remove every single point of failure automatically

options.label: C
options.text: High availability is guaranteed if the VM has enough storage

options.label: D
options.text: Adding more log files automatically creates failover

questions.explanation: Backups help restore after failure; high availability requires redundant active capacity across failure domains.

questions.title: Cloud Computing
questions.prompt: A media service stores millions of user-uploaded images and videos. Files are accessed by object key, need high durability, and do not require mounting a POSIX filesystem. Which storage type is the best fit?

options.label: A
options.text: A single local disk on one application server

options.label: B
options.text: CPU cache

options.label: C
options.text: Object storage

options.label: D
options.text: Browser localStorage as the source of truth

questions.explanation: Object storage is designed for durable, scalable key-based storage of blobs such as images, videos and logs.

questions.title: Cloud Computing
questions.prompt: A reporting service only needs read access to one analytics bucket, but its cloud role can delete buckets, create users and modify network rules. Which principle is violated and what should be done?

options.label: A
options.text: High availability; add a second dashboard color theme

options.label: B
options.text: Vertical scaling; give the service a larger VM

options.label: C
options.text: Eventual consistency; wait longer before reading reports

options.label: D
options.text: Least privilege; reduce the role to only the permissions required for the reporting job

questions.explanation: Least privilege means granting only the permissions needed for the workload and nothing more.

questions.title: Cloud Computing
questions.prompt: A team wants to run short image-thumbnail jobs only when uploads happen. Traffic is bursty, operations overhead should be low, and each job finishes quickly. Which compute model is usually the best starting point?

options.label: A
options.text: Serverless functions triggered by object upload events

options.label: B
options.text: A permanently running large VM that polls every second even when there are no uploads

options.label: C
options.text: Manual execution from a developer laptop for every upload

options.label: D
options.text: A relational database trigger that performs CPU-heavy image processing inside the database engine

questions.explanation: Serverless functions fit short event-driven bursty workloads and reduce always-on infrastructure management.
